mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in 60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of triggered instances or Rank IC stays below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive returns in 50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output that offsets higher borrowing costs, yielding positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains coincide with negative 5-day returns in 60% of tightening periods or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 305 indicate entrenched inflation that forces aggressive central bank tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in 55% of periods when CPI exceeds 305 or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates strong economic momentum and corporate hiring, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in 50% of observations or Rank IC remains negative across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid GDP expansion combined with elevated inflation creates stagflationary pressures that erode real corporate earnings and compress equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDP growth and inflation yields positive returns in 60% of cases or the joint model underperforms single-factor baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production increases while federal funds rates remain stable signal accommodative financial conditions supporting manufacturing output, resulting in positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rising industrial production during stable rate periods produces negative returns in 55% of instances or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward tests.
